refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety constraints) to enable proper allocation and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Measure actual material moved (m³ or ton) and calculate hourly rate. Cross-check against baseline and safety incident log. Verify no safety plan violations occurred during measurement window.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce operational constraints (geofence, speed limits, load limits) without preventing achievement of productivity target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The system shall provide real-time productivity telemetry (material moved, cycle count, idle time) for operator feedback and post-operation analysis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-power hydraulic drivetrain with optimized bucket geometry and load-sensing control to minimize cycle time; couple with low-latency teleoperation and safety-aware task scheduling.
DP-B: Deploy autonomous task execution engine with operator-defined work zones; safety services validate each task before execution; telemetry streams productivity metrics to operator dashboard.

DP-C: Hybrid approach: vehicle equipped with semi-autonomous cycle assistance (dig-load-dump sequences); operator supervises via teleoperation; safety services monitor and override if constraints violated; productivity computed from sensor fusion (load cells, GNSS, IMU).
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid semi-autonomous task execution with operator teleoperation supervision, safety-aware constraint enforcement, and sensor-fusion productivity telemetry.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle cycle control (DP-C mechanical/hydraulic) and teleoperation latency (DP-C network): high latency degrades operator responsiveness and cycle time. Mitigation: local cycle assistance reduces operator intervention frequency.

Coupling between safety constraint enforcement (DP-C safety services) and productivity: overly conservative geofence or speed limits may prevent target cycle time. Mitigation: safety services parameterized by worksite plan; operator can request constraint relaxation with safety manager approval.

Coupling between telemetry accuracy (DP-C sensor fusion) and productivity measurement: poor sensor calibration leads to incorrect KPI reporting. Mitigation: sensor validation protocol before operations.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C balances autonomy (reduces operator workload, improves consistency) with human oversight (maintains safety, allows adaptive response to site conditions). Couplings are manageable via parameterization and local control loops.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle hydraulic system can be retrofitted with load-sensing and cycle-assist logic without major redesign. Risk: Legacy vehicle architecture may not support; mitigation: early prototype validation.

Assumption: Teleoperation network (Tasking, Telemetry) provides <500ms round-trip latency. Risk: Site network congestion or poor coverage degrades operator control. Mitigation: fallback to local operator mode; local cycle assistance reduces latency sensitivity.

Assumption: Safety services can be parameterized per worksite plan without requiring code changes. Risk: Inflexible safety logic forces operational workarounds. Mitigation: safety services designed as configurable rule engine.

Assumption: Sensor fusion (load cells, GNSS, IMU) provides ±5% accuracy for productivity measurement. Risk: Sensor drift or calibration error inflates/deflates reported KPI. Mitigation: daily calibration check; cross-validation with manual spot checks.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but requires explicit specification of: (1) cycle-assist algorithm (which sub-tasks are autonomous vs. operator-controlled), (2) safety constraint parameterization schema, (3) sensor fusion algorithm and accuracy targets, (4) fallback behavior if teleoperation link fails. These should be detailed in next-level DP decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing vehicle, teleoperation, safety services, and their interconnections. Responsible for end-to-end productivity KPI delivery, safety compliance, and operator experience across on-site and remote modes.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Productivity metrics (material moved, cycle count, idle time, equipment state) streamed from vehicle sensors and safety services to operator dashboard and post-operation analytics. Frequency: 1–10 Hz depending on metric.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands (cycle start/stop, bucket angle, travel direction, speed) and safety manager directives (constraint updates, emergency stop) received by vehicle control and safety services. Latency requirement: <500ms for operator responsiveness.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Real-time safety notifications (geofence breach, load limit exceeded, obstacle detected, constraint violation) sent to operator and safety manager. Criticality: high; must be reliable and unambiguous.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying communication substrate (cellular, WiFi, or hybrid) connecting on-site vehicle to remote teleoperation and safety services. Availability and latency are critical for remote operation mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary. FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is inherently a system-level outcome that requires integration of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Containment is maximal. Diffusion risk is minimal (only cross-module interface coordination).

Vehicle owns cycle execution (hydraulics, bucket control, load sensing) and local sensors (load cells, IMU). However, productivity KPI also depends on teleoperation responsiveness and safety constraint enforcement, which are outside Vehicle scope. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for operator interface quality and safety compliance.

Teleoperation owns operator interface and command transmission. However, productivity also depends on vehicle cycle efficiency and safety constraints. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would obscure vehicle performance and safety service contributions.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and safety monitoring. However, productivity KPI is not a safety outcome; it is a business outcome. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services would conflate safety and productivity concerns and create diffusion of responsibility.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: FR-NGHE-001 is a system-level KPI that emerges from the integrated behavior of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. No single sub-module can own productivity without creating diffusion and accountability gaps. NGHE Solution (top-level system) is the appropriate owner. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) will be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a telemetry/analytics module respectively.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary, not a concrete module with code/hardware ownership. Mitigation: Establish a System Integration team or lead responsible for end-to-end KPI verification and cross-module coordination.

Risk: Productivity measurement depends on sensor accuracy and calibration, which may be owned by Vehicle or a separate instrumentation module. Mitigation: Clarify sensor ownership and calibration responsibility in next-level IM assignment for FR-NGHE-001.4.

Risk: Teleoperation latency and safety constraint parameterization are critical to productivity but owned by different modules. Mitigation: Define interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert) with explicit latency and constraint update SLAs.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign FR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution as the system-level owner. Decompose into sub-FRs and allocate to Vehicle (cycle execution), Teleoperation (operator interface), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and a Telemetry/Analytics module (productivity measurement). Establish a System Integration team to own end-to-end KPI verification and interface coordination.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid semi-autonomous task execution with teleoperation supervision and safety-aware constraint enforcement) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 by enabling repeatable cycle execution, operator control, safety compliance, and productivity telemetry. The DP is feasible, non-trivial, and balances autonomy with human oversight. No fundamental mismatch between requirement and solution concept.

diffusion_risks: DP-C cycle-assist logic is distributed across Vehicle (hydraulic control), Teleoperation (operator commands), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Mitigation: Define clear module boundaries and interface contracts (Tasking, SafetyAlert) to minimize coupling and enable independent testing.

Sensor fusion for productivity telemetry may require coordination between Vehicle (sensor hardware) and a separate Telemetry/Analytics module (data processing). Mitigation: Establish Telemetry interface contract with clear data format and update frequency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for FR-NGHE-001 because it encompasses the full system integration scope. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will own sub-FRs and contribute to the overall KPI. No single sub-module can be assigned FR-NGHE-001 without creating diffusion and accountability gaps.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-NGHE-001 is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure material moved (m³ or ton) over agreed period, calculate hourly rate, compare against productivity_target. Verification is feasible and aligns with acceptance criteria. Sub-FRs (cycle time, operator interface responsiveness, safety compliance, telemetry accuracy) are also verifiable via controlled tests and field trials.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution (system-level owner). Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry/Analytics modules. Allocation is clear and traceable. No ambiguity about which module is responsible for which aspect of productivity.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify: (1) which cycle sub-tasks are autonomous vs. operator-controlled, (2) safety constraint parameterization schema, (3) sensor fusion algorithm and accuracy targets, (4) fallback behavior if teleoperation link fails.
Define interface contracts for Tasking (operator commands), SafetyAlert (safety notifications), and Telemetry (productivity metrics) with explicit latency, reliability, and data format requirements.
Establish a System Integration team or lead responsible for end-to-end KPI verification and cross-module coordination.
Plan early prototype validation to confirm vehicle hydraulic system can support cycle-assist logic and sensor integration without major redesign.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system KPI that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (cycle execution, operator interface, safety compliance, telemetry) and allocation to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry/Analytics). The refined FR and selected DP are sound, but further decomposition is necessary to enable detailed design, testing, and accountability. ZIGZAG_DOWN to next level to refine sub-FRs and assign them to sub-modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target, measured under representative site conditions.

FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness (camera, sensor feedback) and control responsiveness (latency <500ms) to sustain target cycle time without excessive operator workload.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce operational constraints (geofence, speed limits, load limits) derived from worksite safety plan without preventing achievement of productivity target; constraint violations shall trigger SafetyAlert.

FR-NGHE-001.4: The system shall measure and report productivity metrics (material moved, cycle count, idle time) with ±5% accuracy via sensor fusion (load cells, GNSS, IMU) and stream telemetry to operator dashboard and post-operation analytics.

assumptions: Vehicle hydraulic system can be retrofitted with load-sensing and cycle-assist logic without major redesign.
Teleoperation network provides <500ms round-trip latency under typical site conditions.
Safety services can be parameterized per worksite plan via configuration (not code changes).
Sensor fusion (load cells, GNSS, IMU) achieves ±5% accuracy for productivity measurement with daily calibration.
Operator (on-site or remote) is trained and authorized to operate NGHE solution per worksite safety plan.
Productivity target is defined by customer and agreed before operations trial.
open_risks: Vehicle architecture may not support cycle-assist logic retrofit; early prototype validation required.
Site network congestion or poor coverage may degrade teleoperation latency; fallback to local operator mode needed.
Safety constraint parameterization may be inflexible or require code changes; safety services design must prioritize configurability.
Sensor drift or calibration error may inflate/deflate reported KPI; daily calibration check and cross-validation with manual spot checks required.
Operator workload in teleoperation mode may be high, reducing cycle time; cycle-assist logic must be tuned to balance autonomy and operator control.
Safety criticality is high; any safety service failure could cause incident; redundancy and fail-safe design required.
evidence_needed: Prototype vehicle with cycle-assist logic and sensor integration; validation of cycle time repeatability and sensor accuracy.
Teleoperation system with operator interface (camera, controls, dashboard); latency measurement under representative site conditions.
Safety services rule engine with parameterization schema; validation of constraint enforcement and SafetyAlert reliability.
Representative operations trial (8-hour shift, 5-day week) with productivity measurement and safety incident log; comparison against productivity_target and baseline.
Operator feedback on interface usability, workload, and situational awareness; post-operation analysis of cycle time, idle time, and constraint violations.
Safety manager review of constraint parameterization and SafetyAlert effectiveness; confirmation of compliance with worksite safety plan.
